summary: Matched the Dolphin DVD waiting queue module by replacing asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.s with src/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.c and switching obj_files.mk to build the C object. The new C source implements the queue sentinel array, interrupt-protected push/pop/check/dequeue helpers, and preserves the assembly behavior closely enough to remove the 160-line assembly file. The PR body only says it was tracked in #480, and there were no review comments.
what_changed: Deleted asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.s, which contained assembly for __DVDClearWaitingQueue, __DVDPushWaitingQueue, __DVDPopWaitingQueue, __DVDCheckWaitingQueue, __DVDDequeueWaitingQueue, and the bss symbol lbl_804A7638.
Added src/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.c with C implementations of the same DVD waiting queue functions.
Introduced a local typedef DVDQueue_sub with two self-referential pointer fields, unk0 and unk4, matching the doubly-linked queue node layout used by the original assembly.
Replaced the anonymous bss label with static DVDQueue_sub WaitingQueue[4], a four-entry sentinel array used by the queue priority/slot loops.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES builds $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.s.o.
__DVDClearWaitingQueue initializes each WaitingQueue entry as an empty circular sentinel by setting both links to itself.
__DVDPushWaitingQueue disables interrupts, inserts a node before the selected queue sentinel tail link, restores interrupts, and returns 1.
__DVDPopWaitingQueue scans four queues, restores/re-disables interrupts before removing the first available node, unlinks it, clears its links to NULL, and returns it; otherwise it returns NULL.
__DVDCheckWaitingQueue scans the four sentinels under disabled interrupts and returns TRUE on the first non-empty queue.
__DVDDequeueWaitingQueue checks both neighboring links for NULL before unlinking a specific node and returning TRUE; if either link is NULL it restores interrupts and returns FALSE.
smart_moves: Modeled the bss queue heads as a static array of four two-pointer structs, directly reflecting the assembly's 0x20-byte lbl_804A7638 region and 8-byte stride.
Used self-referential sentinel nodes for empty queues, which maps cleanly onto the assembly comparisons of WaitingQueue[i].unk0 against &WaitingQueue[i].
Kept OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts calls explicit around list mutations to preserve interrupt-safety and matching call structure.
In __DVDPopWaitingQueue, preserved the unusual restore-then-disable sequence before removal, a likely matching-sensitive control-flow detail from the original assembly.
Used local temporaries named after observed registers in some places, such as r5, r31, and r4, to influence code generation and make the C structure mirror the disassembly.
Kept unresolved field names as unk0 and unk4 instead of overclaiming semantics not proven in this PR slice.
decomp_lessons: Small assembly modules with simple bss-backed data structures can often be matched by identifying stride, sentinel behavior, and pointer writes before attempting semantic renaming.
A bss label of size 0x20 with repeated operations at offsets 0, 8, 0x10, and 0x18 is strong evidence for an array of four 8-byte structs.
For linked-list queue code, matching may depend on preserving the original order of pointer assignments, not just the final logical state.
Interrupt save/restore variables should use BOOL when matching Dolphin OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts patterns.
Do not simplify apparently redundant interrupt transitions if the assembly shows them; the restore/re-disable sequence in __DVDPopWaitingQueue is part of the matched behavior.
When exact struct semantics are not established, local private typedefs with unk field names are acceptable stepping stones for matching.
naming_conventions: Internal Dolphin DVD functions retain double-underscore names: __DVDClearWaitingQueue, __DVDPushWaitingQueue, __DVDPopWaitingQueue, __DVDCheckWaitingQueue, __DVDDequeueWaitingQueue.
The private queue node typedef was named DVDQueue_sub, with struct tag _DVDQueue_sub.
Unknown pointer fields were named by offset, unk0 and unk4.
The former bss label lbl_804A7638 was replaced by a descriptive static symbol, WaitingQueue.
The interrupt state local was named intrEnabled, matching common Dolphin decomp style for OSDisableInterrupts return values.
Boolean-returning functions use BOOL with TRUE and FALSE from Dolphin headers rather than raw int literals where the source signature indicates boolean behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the assembly object to a C object in obj_files.mk only after adding the matching C implementation.
Matched the 4-iteration assembly loops using for (i = 0; i < 4; i++).
Represented the assembly's 8-byte queue element stride as a struct containing two pointers.
Preserved the linked-list insertion order in __DVDPushWaitingQueue: update old tail forward link, set new node back link, set new node forward link to sentinel, then update sentinel back link.
Preserved __DVDPopWaitingQueue's scan of four queue heads and early return path for the first non-empty queue.
Cleared popped node links to NULL after restoring interrupts, matching the assembly's post-unlink stores.
Used local register-like variable names in matching-sensitive areas to guide compiler output.
Included <dolphin/dvd/dvd.h> and <dolphin/os/OSInterrupt.h> to obtain DVD-related declarations, BOOL, OSDisableInterrupts, and OSRestoreInterrupts.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states: "Tracked in #480."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename DVDQueue_sub fields unk0 and unk4 to semantic next/prev names.
Search for other Dolphin DVD queue or command block structs that include the same two-link layout.
Check issue #480 for the broader tracking context of Dolphin DVD matching work.
Look for later changes to dvdqueue.c that add headers, prototypes, or replace the private typedef with a shared DVD struct.
